name: Karnofsky Performance Status Scale
description: An assessment tool for functional impairment used to understand patient needs, ability to carry out daily activities, and to assess patient prognosis. Widely used in oncology and palliative care settings.
instructions: Select the description that best represents the patient's current functional status and ability to carry out daily activities.

questions.question: Select the patient's current Karnofsky Performance Status:

options.label: 100% - No complaints; no evidence of disease
options.category: Able to carry on normal activity and to work. No special care needed.

options.label: 90% - Able to carry on normal activity; minor signs or symptoms of disease
options.category: Able to carry on normal activity and to work. No special care needed.

options.label: 80% - Normal activity with effort; some signs or symptoms of disease
options.category: Able to carry on normal activity and to work. No special care needed.

options.label: 70% - Cares for self; unable to carry on normal activity or to do active work
options.category: Unable to work; able to live at home and care for most personal needs; varying amount of assistance needed.

options.label: 60% - Requires occasional assistance but is able to care for most personal needs
options.category: Unable to work; able to live at home and care for most personal needs; varying amount of assistance needed.

options.label: 50% - Requires considerable assistance and frequent medical care
options.category: Unable to work; able to live at home and care for most personal needs; varying amount of assistance needed.

options.label: 40% - Disabled; requires special care and assistance
options.category: Unable to care for self; requires equivalent of institutional or hospital care; disease may be progressing rapidly.

options.label: 30% - Severely disabled; hospital admission indicated although death not imminent
options.category: Unable to care for self; requires equivalent of institutional or hospital care; disease may be progressing rapidly.

options.label: 20% - Very sick; hospital admission necessary; active supportive treatment necessary
options.category: Unable to care for self; requires equivalent of institutional or hospital care; disease may be progressing rapidly.

options.label: 10% - Moribund; fatal processes progressing rapidly
options.category: Unable to care for self; requires equivalent of institutional or hospital care; disease may be progressing rapidly.

severityBands.label: Fully Functional
severityBands.description: Able to carry on normal activity and to work. No special care needed.

severityBands.label: Ambulatory / Unable to Work
severityBands.description: Unable to work; able to live at home and care for most personal needs; varying amount of assistance needed.

severityBands.label: Disabled / Requires Care
severityBands.description: Unable to care for self; requires equivalent of institutional or hospital care.

severityBands.label: Critical / End of Life
severityBands.description: Very sick or moribund; fatal processes may be progressing rapidly.

interpretation.scoringMethod: Single selection from 0% to 100% in 10% increments. Higher scores indicate better functional status.

clinicalCategories.ableToWork: 80-100%: Able to carry on normal activity and work
clinicalCategories.unableToWork: 50-70%: Unable to work but can live at home with varying assistance
clinicalCategories.unableToCareSelf: 10-40%: Unable to care for self, requires institutional/hospital care

interpretation.prognosticValue: KPS is a strong predictor of survival and treatment tolerance in various disease states, particularly in oncology.
interpretation.clinicalUse: Used to determine eligibility for clinical trials, guide treatment decisions, and estimate prognosis.

references.citation: Karnofsky DA, Burchenal JH. The clinical evaluation of chemotherapeutic agents in cancer. In: MacLeod CM, ed. Evaluation of Chemotherapeutic Agents. New York: Columbia University Press; 1949:191-205.
Schag CC, Heinrich RL, Ganz PA. Karnofsky performance status revisited: reliability, validity, and guidelines. J Clin Oncol. 1984;2(3):187-193.
